stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Running through the sprinkler, ______

choices.text: the children laughed in the backyard.
the backyard was where the children laughed.
laughter came from the children in the backyard.
it was the children laughing in the backyard.

explanation: The introductory participial phrase 'Running through the sprinkler' must modify the noun that follows it; only 'the children' could be doing the running.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Hiking along the ridge, ______

choices.text: a bald eagle was spotted by Maya circling above.
Maya spotted a bald eagle circling above.
there was a bald eagle that Maya spotted circling above.
the bald eagle circling above caught Maya's eye.

explanation: The opening phrase needs a human subject right after the comma; Maya is the one hiking, so she must appear first in the main clause.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

While reading the newspaper, ______

choices.text: the coffee grew cold on the table next to Damian.
Damian let his coffee grow cold on the table.
there was cold coffee sitting next to Damian.
it was Damian whose coffee grew cold.

explanation: The phrase 'While reading the newspaper' needs an agent who can read; Damian must come first in the main clause, not the coffee.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Founded in 1912, ______

choices.text: tourists today still visit the small seaside town.
many tourists still come to the small seaside town.
there are still tourists visiting the small seaside town.
the small seaside town still draws tourists today.

explanation: The phrase 'Founded in 1912' describes what was founded; the town, not the tourists, must follow the comma.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Exhausted after the long flight, ______

choices.text: the hotel bed looked perfect to Priya.
collapsing onto the hotel bed happened immediately.
Priya collapsed onto the hotel bed.
it was a hotel bed that Priya needed.

explanation: Only a person can be 'exhausted after the long flight'; Priya must be the subject that follows the opening modifier.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

A self-taught astronomer and mathematician, ______

choices.text: Benjamin Banneker gained wide readership with his almanacs in the 1790s.
Benjamin Banneker's almanacs gained wide readership in the 1790s.
the almanacs of Benjamin Banneker gained wide readership in the 1790s.
wide readership was gained by Benjamin Banneker's almanacs in the 1790s.

explanation: The appositive describes a person, so 'Benjamin Banneker' himself must follow the comma, not his almanacs or their readership.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Built with locally quarried limestone, ______

choices.text: the cathedral took workers just under a decade to complete.
workers completed the cathedral in just under a decade.
just under a decade was needed to complete the cathedral.
completing the cathedral took workers just under a decade.

explanation: Only the cathedral could have been 'built with locally quarried limestone'; it must be the subject that follows the introductory phrase.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Known for her precise brushwork and restrained palette, ______

choices.text: painter Agnes Martin drew comparisons to Minimalist sculptors of the 1960s.
comparisons to Minimalist sculptors of the 1960s were drawn about Agnes Martin.
the paintings of Agnes Martin drew comparisons to Minimalist sculptors of the 1960s.
it was Agnes Martin whose work drew comparisons to Minimalist sculptors of the 1960s.

explanation: The phrase describes a person known for her technique; Agnes Martin herself must be the subject of the main clause, not her paintings or comparisons.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

After circling the harbor for nearly an hour, ______

choices.text: the seaplane pilot finally found a safe landing spot.
a safe landing spot was finally found by the seaplane pilot.
there was finally a safe landing spot for the seaplane pilot.
finding a safe landing spot was the seaplane pilot's goal.

explanation: Only the pilot could have circled the harbor; the pilot must be the subject that follows the introductory phrase, not a landing spot.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

A fierce advocate for public libraries, ______

choices.text: Andrew Carnegie funded the construction of thousands of them across the country.
millions of dollars were donated by Andrew Carnegie to build them.
the construction of thousands of libraries was funded by Andrew Carnegie.
thousands of them were funded across the country by Andrew Carnegie.

explanation: An 'advocate' is a person; Andrew Carnegie must follow the comma, not the libraries or the money.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Written entirely in second person, ______

choices.text: Jay McInerney's novel 'Bright Lights, Big City' addresses readers directly.
readers of Jay McInerney's novel 'Bright Lights, Big City' feel directly addressed.
Jay McInerney addresses readers directly in 'Bright Lights, Big City.'
'Bright Lights, Big City,' Jay McInerney's novel, makes readers feel addressed directly.

explanation: Only the novel itself could be 'written entirely in second person'; the novel must be the subject of the main clause.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Frustrated by the slow pace of the experiment, ______

choices.text: a new protocol was drafted by the lab team.
drafting a new protocol was the lab team's next step.
there was a new protocol drafted by the lab team.
the lab team drafted a new protocol.

explanation: 'Frustrated' must describe a person or group that can feel frustration; the lab team needs to be the subject right after the comma.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Designed to survive the harsh Martian atmosphere, ______

choices.text: engineers tested the new rover for over a year in simulated conditions.
the testing of the new rover took over a year in simulated conditions.
the new rover underwent over a year of testing in simulated conditions.
over a year of testing in simulated conditions was required for the new rover.

explanation: The rover was designed for Mars, so the rover must follow the comma as the subject, not the engineers or the testing process.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Trained as a classical violinist before turning to jazz, ______

choices.text: Regina Carter's improvisations reflect a rare technical discipline.
a rare technical discipline shows in Regina Carter's improvisations.
the improvisations of Regina Carter show rare technical discipline.
Regina Carter brings a rare technical discipline to her improvisations.

explanation: Only a person can be 'trained as a classical violinist'; Regina Carter herself, not her improvisations, must follow the opening modifier.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Stacked neatly on the kitchen counter, ______

choices.text: Teodoro noticed the envelopes had gone unopened for weeks.
the envelopes had gone unopened for weeks.
Teodoro hadn't opened the envelopes in weeks.
weeks had passed with the envelopes unopened by Teodoro.

explanation: Only the envelopes could be 'stacked neatly on the kitchen counter'; they must be the subject directly following the modifier.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Having spent two decades studying the vocalizations of sperm whales off Dominica, ______

choices.text: one of the largest acoustic archives of the species has been compiled by biologist Shane Gero.
biologist Shane Gero's acoustic archive of the species is one of the largest ever compiled.
biologist Shane Gero has compiled one of the largest acoustic archives of the species.
the acoustic archive that biologist Shane Gero compiled is one of the largest of the species.

explanation: 'Having spent two decades studying' requires a human agent; Shane Gero himself, not his archive, must be the subject of the main clause.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Assembled from fragments recovered at three separate dig sites in eastern Ethiopia, ______

choices.text: paleontologists argue that the skeleton represents a previously unknown hominin species.
the skeleton is argued by paleontologists to represent a previously unknown hominin species.
it has been argued by paleontologists that the skeleton represents a previously unknown hominin species.
a previously unknown hominin species is what paleontologists argue the skeleton represents.

explanation: Only the skeleton could have been 'assembled from fragments'; it must be the grammatical subject following the opening modifier, even though the sentence is in the passive voice.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Drawing on archival letters that had been overlooked by previous biographers, ______

choices.text: a more complicated portrait of the poet emerges in Kaveh Akbar's study.
Kaveh Akbar's study paints a more complicated portrait of the poet.
the poet is given a more complicated portrait in Kaveh Akbar's study.
Kaveh Akbar paints a more complicated portrait of the poet in his study.

explanation: Only a person can 'draw on archival letters'; Kaveh Akbar himself, not his study, must be the subject that follows the introductory phrase.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

Engineered to fold into a shape smaller than a deck of cards, ______

choices.text: the miniature satellite can be deployed from a standard launch canister without modification.
researchers can deploy the miniature satellite from a standard launch canister.
deploying the miniature satellite from a standard launch canister is straightforward for researchers.
a standard launch canister can deploy the miniature satellite without modification.

explanation: The satellite, not the researchers or the canister, was 'engineered to fold'; the satellite must be the subject directly following the opening modifier.

stem:
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

A pioneering figure in early-twentieth-century modern dance whose choreography drew on Indigenous ceremonial movement, ______

choices.text: the performances of Rosalia Chladek influenced generations of European dancers.
Rosalia Chladek influenced generations of European dancers through her performances.
generations of European dancers were influenced by Rosalia Chladek's performances.
European dance was shaped for generations by Rosalia Chladek's performances.

explanation: The lengthy appositive describes a person (a 'pioneering figure'); Rosalia Chladek herself must follow the comma, not her performances or the dancers she influenced.
